summary.title: Student Exam Management System Threat Model
summary.description: Threat model for a hypothetical web-based exam platform supporting student exam taking, lecturer exam authoring and grading, and administrator user and audit management. Federated authentication via a school identity provider

data.name: Internet (untrusted)
data.description: Highest threat exposure. Contains all external entities. All traffic entering or leaving this zone crosses the public internet and must be encrypted and authenticated. Primary attack surface for spoofing, credential theft, and injection.

data.name: Web tier (semi-trusted)
data.description: Public-facing application layer. Handles authentication, session management, and federation. Elevated risk due to direct internet exposure. Enforces MFA, rate limiting, and HSTS.

data.name: Service tier (trusted)
data.description: Internal application services with no direct internet exposure. Communicates via TLS over private network segments. Processes business logic for exam delivery, grading, authoring, and administration.

data.name: Data tier (most trusted)
data.description: Most sensitive zone. Stores credentials, exam content, student submissions, grades, and the audit log. Access restricted to specific service accounts with least-privilege database roles. Encrypted at rest.

data.name: Student
data.description: End user who takes exams within a fixed time window. Untrusted source of input. All data received from this actor is validated before processing.

data.name: Lecturer
data.description: Authenticated staff user who authors exams, edits answer keys, and enters manual grades. Higher privilege than students; insider-threat vector for answer-key manipulation.

data.name: Admin
data.description: Privileged operator who manages user accounts and reads the audit log. Insider-threat target; actions must be fully audited and access restricted to the minimum required.

data.name: School Identity Provider (SSO)
data.description: External system that authenticates staff and student credentials and returns a cryptographically signed identity assertion. Treated as semi-trusted; assertion signatures are always verified.

data.name: User and Audit Administration
data.description: Exposes privileged admin operations: create or disable accounts, assign roles, and read audit log entries. All actions are written to DS4. Elevation-of-privilege risk if authorisation checks are absent.

threats.title: IDOR on role-assignment endpoint allows self-promotion
threats.description: The admin role-assignment endpoint takes a user ID and a target role from the request body. If the server-side authorisation check is missing or only validates session presence rather than role, a logged-in student can issue a request that promotes their own account to lecturer or admin.
threats.mitigation: Server-side authorisation check that resolves the caller's role on every request and rejects role-changing actions for non-admins; deny by default; canonical authorisation helper used by every privileged endpoint; access-control test suite executed in CI on every change to authorisation code

data.name: Exam Authoring
data.description: Allows lecturers to create, edit, and publish exams and answer keys in DS2. Pre-publish edits are unrestricted; post-publish edits require dual-control approval and produce an audit event.

threats.title: Pre-exam answer key exposure via insecure direct object reference
threats.description: A student who discovers the exam ID can request the exam-authoring API endpoint and retrieve the answer key before the exam window opens. The endpoint does not enforce role separation between the authoring-write and grading-read paths.
threats.mitigation: Enforce role-based access control on all exam-authoring endpoints; answer keys must only be readable by the grading service account and the owning lecturer; all answer-key reads produce audit events; keys are stored encrypted at rest and decrypted only inside the grading service.

data.name: Grading
data.description: Applies the answer key to MCQ and short-answer submissions, accepts lecturer-entered marks for open-ended answers, computes the final grade, and publishes it to the student via DS3.

threats.title: Grade tampering via direct DS3 write from compromised grading service
threats.description: An attacker who compromises the grading service account can issue arbitrary UPDATE statements against DS3, altering grades without going through the normal grading workflow. There is no secondary approval mechanism for grade changes.
threats.mitigation: Separate DB accounts per service; grading service account has no direct UPDATE on final-grade columns; grade changes must go through an audited stored procedure; all writes produce audit events.

data.name: Exam Delivery
data.description: Loads an active exam for a logged-in student, serves questions, and enforces the per-student countdown timer. Read-only against DS2; writes nothing to the exam bank.

threats.title: Concurrent exam-start storm exhausts the application thread pool
threats.description: Several thousand students start the same exam at the same scheduled minute. Each session triggers a synchronous read of the entire exam from DS2, exhausting the application thread pool and the database connection pool. Legitimate students cannot start the exam.
threats.mitigation: Pre-fetch exam content into per-session cache at seat allocation; auto-scale on connection-pool saturation; connect-time admission control (queue with progress feedback) when capacity is exceeded; capacity tested against the largest scheduled cohort plus 50% headroom.

data.name: Auth and Session Management
data.description: Validates credentials, brokers OIDC/SAML federation with the school identity provider, issues and refreshes session tokens, enforces MFA, and writes authentication events to the audit log.

threats.title: Credential stuffing during open exam window
threats.description: Attackers replay leaked passwords from public breach corpora against the login endpoint during a scheduled exam window. A successful match grants a session that is indistinguishable from a legitimate student's. The attack is high-volume and timed to maximise impact.
threats.mitigation: Mandatory MFA for all roles; per-account and per-IP rate limiting on /login; CAPTCHA after N failed attempts; anomaly-detection alert on geographic or device deltas during exam windows; integration with breached-password APIs (HIBP) at password-set time.

data.name: session token (cookie)
data.description: Short-lived, HttpOnly, Secure cookie issued to the authenticated user after successful login or token refresh.

data.name: federation request
data.description: Outbound HTTPS request from P1 to the school IdP initiating the OIDC/SAML federation handshake.

data.name: session token (cookie)
data.description: Short-lived, HttpOnly, Secure cookie issued to the authenticated user after successful login or token refresh.

data.name: session token (cookie)
data.description: Short-lived, HttpOnly, Secure cookie issued to the authenticated user after successful login or token refresh.

data.name: login (credentials)
data.description: User-supplied username and password submitted over HTTPS to the authentication endpoint.

data.name: login (credentials)
data.description: User-supplied username and password submitted over HTTPS to the authentication endpoint.

data.name: signed identity assertion
data.description: SAML assertion or OIDC id_token returned by the IdP, cryptographically signed with the IdP private key.

threats.title: Tampering with the SSO identity assertion
threats.description: A man-in-the-middle on a compromised network modifies the SAML or OIDC assertion in transit, swapping the asserted identity before P1 consumes it. If P1 does not validate the issuer signature, audience, and nonce, the attacker logs in as any user.
threats.mitigation: HTTPS with HSTS preload; full validation of issuer signature, audience, nbf/exp, and per-request nonce; reject assertions over plain HTTP; pin the IdP signing key.

data.name: login (credentials)
data.description: User-supplied username and password submitted over HTTPS to the authentication endpoint.

data.name: fetch exam
data.description: Authenticated student request to retrieve the active exam content for the current session window.

data.name: submission acknowledgement
data.description: HTTP 200 response confirming the student's submission was received, validated, and persisted.

data.name: exam questions
data.description: Encrypted exam content (questions, options, timer metadata) delivered to the authenticated student.

data.name: manage users / read audit
data.description: Admin-authenticated request to perform user lifecycle operations or retrieve audit log entries.

data.name: manage users
data.description: Internal TLS call from P6 to DS1 to create, suspend, or update user account records.

data.name: read / write exam content
data.description: Lecturer-authenticated read and write operations on exam questions and answer keys in DS2.

data.name: read answer key
data.description: Internal TLS read by the grading service to retrieve the answer key for a specific published exam.

data.name: read / write grades
data.description: Internal TLS read and write by the grading service on student submission records and computed grades in DS3.

data.name: write submission
data.description: Internal TLS write of a validated, timestamped, and hashed student submission record to DS3.

data.name: read exam content
data.description: Internal TLS read by the exam delivery service to retrieve active exam content from DS2.

data.name: append audit event
data.description: Append-only TLS write of a structured audit event (actor, action, timestamp, resource) to DS4.

data.name: append audit event
data.description: Append-only TLS write of a structured audit event (actor, action, timestamp, resource) to DS4.

data.name: read audit log
data.description: Internal TLS read by the admin service to retrieve audit entries from DS4 for review.

data.name: Data Flow
data.description: Internal data flow. See source and target elements for full context.

data.name: author / edit exam
data.description: Lecturer-authenticated HTTP request to create or modify exam content, questions, or the answer key.

data.name: enter manual grade
data.description: Lecturer-authenticated HTTP request to submit or update a manual mark for an open-ended answer.

data.name: Identity Store
data.description: Hashed passwords, MFA seeds, role assignments, and account status. Encryption at rest required; credentials must be stored as salted hashes (bcrypt/Argon2). Access restricted to the authentication service account.

data.name: Audit log
data.description: Append-only log of authentication, authoring, submission, grading, and admin events. Each entry is hash-linked to the previous for tamper-evidence. Write-once; the application service account has INSERT only — no UPDATE or DELETE.

threats.title: Insider with database admin rights deletes audit entries
threats.description: A database administrator, or an attacker who has compromised the DBA account, deletes or rewrites entries in DS4 to cover unauthorised actions. Because the audit log lives in the same database as the data it audits, a single privileged actor can both act and erase the trace.
threats.mitigation: Ship audit events to a separate trust zone (write-once medium or external SIEM) on creation; append-only schema enforced by database privileges (REVOKE DELETE, REVOKE UPDATE); periodic hash anchoring of the audit chain to an immutable store; separation of duties between database administrator and application owner roles.

data.name: Exam bank
data.description: Exam metadata, questions, and answer keys. Answer keys are stored encrypted and are only decrypted inside the grading service. Pre-exam confidentiality is critical; unauthorised read before the window opens constitutes cheating.

threats.title: Pre-exam answer key exposure via direct URL access
threats.description: Provide a desA student who discovers or guesses the exam resource URL (e.g. `/api/exams/{id}/answers`) can request the answer key directly before the exam window opens. The exam-authoring endpoint that lecturers use to read the key shares the same route or an adjacent one, and if the server-side role check is absent or checks only authentication (session present) rather than authorisation (role = lecturer or grader), any authenticated student can retrieve the full answer key in plaintext before sitting the exam. The exam ID is often sequential or derivable from the student's own exam-start response, making discovery low-effort.cription for this threat
threats.mitigation: Provide remediaEnforce role-based access control on all DS2 read paths — the answer key columns must only be readable by the grading service account (P4.1) and the owning lecturer (P5), never by the student role; separate the student-facing exam-content endpoint from the answer-key endpoint at the routing layer; answer keys are stored encrypted with a key that is only released to P4.1 after the exam window closes; all answer-key reads produce an audit event in DS4 (Level 1) / DS3c (Level 2); alert on any answer-key read that occurs before the window-open timestamption for this threat or a reason if status is N/A

data.name: Submissions and grades
data.description: Per-student answers, content hashes, marks, and final grades. Contains student PII. Column-level encryption on PII fields; access is role-separated between the submission, grading, and publishing service accounts.

threats.title: Mass disclosure of grades and student PII via lateral movement
threats.description: An attacker who has compromised a low-privilege application account pivots laterally to the database account used by the grading service and exfiltrates the entire student PII and grade record set in one query.
threats.mitigation: Column-level encryption for student PII; database role separation (grading service has read on grades only, not on PII); parameterised queries throughout; principle of least privilege on the database account; egress alerting on bulk reads from sensitive tables.

data.name: Submission Handler
data.description: Receives student answers over HTTPS, validates schema and exam window ownership, sanitises free-text input, and persists the submission to DS3 with a timestamp and a content hash.
